key_insight: Small-radius, high-electronegativity A-site dopants such as Ca can simultaneously improve proton transport and chemical stability in BaCeO3-based perovskite electrolytes by weakening A–O bonding and contracting the lattice.
engineering_relevance: A-site doping offers a practical design route for more durable proton-conducting solid oxide fuel cell electrolytes with better proton uptake, lower impurity formation, and reduced sensitivity to acidic gases.
method: Mechanistic analysis of A-site dopant effects on bonding, vacancy formation, and proton behavior
Assessment of chemical stability trends, including CO2/SO2 adsorption and grain-boundary effects
evidence_signal: The abstract reports computational evidence that Ca doping improves proton uptake, suppresses impurity phases, weakens acid-gas adsorption, and can reduce grain-boundary resistance.
watch_item: Benefits may depend on whether proton transfer is the rate-limiting step, and excessive A-site segregation or composition changes could introduce microstructural tradeoffs.
paper_title: Role of small-radius and high-electronegativity A-Site dopants in enhancing proton transport and stability of perovskite electrolytes
